title: EU CBAM Definitive Phase
description: The EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism enters its definitive phase with financial liability for importers.
summary: The world's first carbon border tax went live on January 1, 2026. EU importers of cement, aluminium, fertilisers, iron and steel, hydrogen, and electricity must now purchase CBAM certificates priced to the EU ETS carbon market. The European Commission published the first quarterly certificate price at €75.36 per tonne of CO2 for Q1 2026. Russia filed a formal WTO dispute in May 2025; the EU declined to enter consultations.
insight: CBAM turns carbon leakage policy into a real import cost for EU supply chains, not just a reporting regime.
